name: The Ulldecona Reservoir - Circular via La Pobla de Benifassà and El Ballestar
name_original: El Embalse de Ulldecona – Circular por La Pobla de Benifassà y El Ballestar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Pobla De Benifassà are situated within the La Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, a region characterized by rugged mountains, verdant woodlands, and the Ulldecona Reservoir. The terrain offers varied topography, including challenging climbs and scenic routes connecting historic villages. This protected area, part of the Puertos de Beceite massif, features expansive forests and diverse natural environments. The elevation changes throughout the region provide a range of experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around La Pobla De Benifassà The most popular touring cycling route is The Ulldecona Reservoir - Circular via La Pobla de Benifassà and El Ballestar, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Ulldecona Reservoir and passes through local villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ulldecona Reservoir – Faig Pare loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona, a difficult 35.2 miles (56.7 km) path. It traverses varied terrain, including sections near the Ulldecona Reservoir and through forested areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Along the riu Verd – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from la Pobla de Benifassà, a 24.2 miles (38.9 km) trail leading through the riu Verd and Ulldecona Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Touring cycling around La Pobla De Benifassà is defined by rugged mountains, verdant woodlands, and the Ulldecona Reservoir. The network primarily offers challenging routes, with 13 difficult trails out of 14 total. The routes in La Pobla De Benifassà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Benifassà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: The touring cycling routes around La Pobla de Benifassà are predominantly challenging. Out of 14 available routes, 13 are classified as difficult, with only one moderate option. This region is characterized by rugged mountains and significant elevation changes, making it ideal for experienced cyclists seeking demanding rides.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: While most touring cycling routes in La Pobla de Benifassà are rated as difficult, there is one moderate route available. Cyclists new to the area or looking for a less strenuous experience should be prepared for varied terrain and elevation, even on the moderate option, as the region is known for its mountainous landscape.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover while touring cycling in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: The region offers stunning natural beauty. You can cycle past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, known for its emerald to turquoise waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another picturesque spot. The area is within the La Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, featuring expansive forests and diverse wildlife.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, the region blends natural exploration with cultural heritage. You can find the historic Monasterio de Santa María de Benifassà, one of the first convents in the Valencian Community. Near the Ulldecona Reservoir, the Cova dels Rossegadors features ancient cave paintings, part of a UNESCO World Heritage site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of Saints Abdón and Senén (Fredes)&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable landmark.

Q: Can I cycle to a waterfall in the region?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a unique and picturesque 30-meter high waterfall that can be reached by bike. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Salt de Robert Waterfall loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes this natural highlight.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer views of the Ulldecona Reservoir?
A: Several routes provide excellent views of the Ulldecona Reservoir. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934199072/the-ulldecona-reservoir-circular-via-la-pobla-de-benifassa-and-el-ballestar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Ulldecona Reservoir - Circular via La Pobla de Benifassà and El Ballestar&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Faig Pare loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the riu Verd – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from la Pobla de Benifassà&lt;/a&gt;. These routes traverse varied terrain, often running alongside or offering panoramic views of the reservoir.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in La Pobla de Benifassà are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934199072/the-ulldecona-reservoir-circular-via-la-pobla-de-benifassa-and-el-ballestar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Ulldecona Reservoir - Circular via La Pobla de Benifassà and El Ballestar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Along the riu Verd – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from la Pobla de Benifassà&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is La Pobla de Benifassà a good destination for family touring cycling?
A: While the region's natural beauty is appealing, most touring cycling routes in La Pobla de Benifassà are classified as difficult due to rugged terrain and significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is noted as a family-friendly site, but families with younger children or less experienced cyclists should carefully review route details and consider the challenging nature of the trails before planning a touring cycling trip.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the breathtaking views, the deep connection with nature offered by the expansive forests, and the challenging yet rewarding terrain within the La Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: Given the region's varied topography and potential for challenging climbs, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in exposed areas, while winters may bring colder temperatures and potentially more challenging conditions in higher elevations.

Q: Are there specific permits required for cycling within La Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for cycling on established routes within La Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to check the official park regulations before your visit, especially if you plan to venture off marked trails or engage in activities that might require special permission.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Pobla de Benifassà?
A: You can expect a diverse and often challenging terrain. Routes feature rugged mountains, verdant woodlands, and sections near the Ulldecona Reservoir. There's a mix of paved and gravel sections, with many routes involving significant elevation changes and climbs, catering to cyclists who enjoy a hard gravel ride or a demanding road tour.
tips: Located in the municipality of Puebla de Benifasar, the Ulldecona reservoir receives water from the Fou ravine (or Sénia river), the Teulatí ravine, the Tenalla ravine and the Pobla ravine (or riu Verd). The views from the bridge are magnificent and the color of the water varies according to the weather, with emerald or turquoise shades.
A panoramic point on the road that offers beautiful views towards the town of El Ballestar. A good place to take a break and appreciate the postcards that the landscape gives away!
Small town in the interior of Castellón with typical buildings, many of them in ruins and others very well preserved. It has some services although you will only find the basics there.
